title: azure-kv: put() ignores field selector, corrupts secret
description: AzureKvBackend stores field: Option<String> but put() never checks it. If constructed with ?field=password (meaning: extract 'password' from a JSON secret), put('new-pass') overwrites the entire Azure KV secret with 'new-pass'. The next get() tries to parse 'new-pass' as JSON → DecodeFailed. Fix: add if self.field.is_some() { return Err(SecretError::InvalidUri(...)) } at the start of put(), same as aws-sm.
